reasoning_details.text:
We need to translate the given markdown content into Chinese, preserving structure, code blocks, MDX components, etc. Also need to adjust relative URLs to have ../ prefix. The original relative URLs: in blockquote link? There's a link to https://datatracker.ietf.org/... that's absolute, no change. Images: ./hero-wide.webp, ./square-200.webp, ./inline-url-diagram-dark.svg, ./hero-concept-8-drawers.webp. Need to prefix with ../. So change to ../hero-wide.webp etc. Also any other relative URLs: none.

Frontmatter: need to translate title, subTitle, maybe cover_alt, cover_credit (none). language field not present; we need to add language? The spec says if a language field exists, set it to "Chinese". Not required to add. Keep other fields unchanged. Translate title and subTitle values. Keep other keys unchanged.

So frontmatter becomes:

---
title: "是时候使用 llm:// 连接字符串了"
subTitle: "使用 `llm://` URL 简化模型与提供商配置"
date: 2026-01-30
modified: 2026-02-26
tags: [ai, llm, api, developer-experience, standards]
category: AI
draft: false
popularity: 1
social_image: desktop-social.webp
cover_full_width: ./hero-wide.webp
cover_mobile: ./square-200.webp
cover_icon: ./square-200.webp
---

But need to keep relative URLs with ../ prefix? Those are for cover images? The rule: "Preserve ALL relative URL paths for images, CSS, links, or other resources should prefix/prepend with a `../`." That includes cover_full_width etc. Those are file paths. So change to ../hero-wide.webp etc. So:

cover_full_width: ../hero-wide.webp
cover_mobile: ../square-200.webp
cover_icon: ../square-200.webp

Also social_image maybe relative? It's desktop-social.webp, also relative. Should prefix: ../desktop-social.webp

Now inside content, the blockquote with class "inset" contains a link. Keep as is.

Image markdown: ![the parts of a LLM connection string](./inline-url-diagram-dark.svg) => change to ../inline-url-diagram-dark.svg

Another image: ![a messy env var drawer](./hero-concept-8-drawers.webp) => ../hero-concept-8-drawers.webp

Also any other relative URLs? None.

Now translate prose into Chinese, preserving tone. Keep code blocks unchanged.

Also need to translate any inline code spans? Those are code, keep unchanged.

Translate blockquote text content: "Update: This article led to an [Internet-Draft for the `llm://` URI scheme](...)." Should translate "Update:" and maybe rest. Keep link text? The link text is "Internet-Draft for the `llm://` URI scheme". Translate that phrase. So: "更新：本文促成了一个针对 `llm://` URI 方案的 [Internet-Draft]..." but link text should be translated. So we translate link text but keep URL.

Similarly other blockquotes: "One string with everything from your **auth** to your **endpoint** to your **hyperparameters**." Translate.

Also AI-response inset blockquote: "The database world took decades to figure this out.\nGood news, in AI timelines, that's only about half a vibe-year ago." Translate.

Also the footnote comment: {/* ¹ Yes, I know that `URI` is more correct than `URL`. If you are pedantic enough to actually care about that distinction, please go touch grass. */} This is a comment, not displayed. Should we translate? It's inside comment, but it's still part of source. Probably keep as is, but translation not required? The instruction: translate only reader-facing prose. This comment is not displayed, but it's part of source code. Might be considered not reader-facing, so keep unchanged. We'll leave as is.

Now translate paragraphs.

Let's produce final output.
